description: Questions for the Geometry Foundation Journey progression

class7_square_parts_001.subject: Mathematics
class7_square_parts_001.question_text: A square is divided into 4 equal triangles by drawing lines from the center. What fraction of the square is each triangle?
class7_square_parts_001.explanation: A square divided into 4 equal parts means each part is 1/4 of the whole.

solution_steps.description: Draw diagonals from center to each corner of the square
Count the number of equal triangles formed: 4
Each triangle is 1 out of 4 equal parts = 1/4

why_wrong_explanations.0: 1/2 would mean only 2 parts, but drawing from center to all corners creates 4 equal triangles.
why_wrong_explanations.2: 1/3 would mean 3 parts, but a square divides into 4 triangles from the center.
why_wrong_explanations.3: 1/8 is too small. Drawing from center to corners creates exactly 4 equal triangles, not 8.

rescue_foundation_square_center_angle_00.subject: Mathematics
rescue_foundation_square_center_angle_00.question_text: A square is divided from its center into 4 equal triangles. What is the measure of each central angle?
rescue_foundation_square_center_angle_00.explanation: A full circle is 360°. Dividing by 4 equal parts gives 90°.

solution_steps.description: A full circle around a point measures 360°
A square divides the circle into 4 equal central angles
Each central angle = Total angle ÷ Number of parts

why_wrong_explanations.0: 45° would be for an octagon, not a square. A square has 4 equal parts.
why_wrong_explanations.2: 180° is a straight angle, not the central angle of a square.
why_wrong_explanations.3: 360° is the total angle around a point, not each individual central angle.

rescue_bridge_hexagon_center_angle_001.subject: Mathematics
rescue_bridge_hexagon_center_angle_001.question_text: A regular hexagon has a side length of 6 cm. Find the area of one of the central triangles formed by drawing lines from the center to the vertices.
rescue_bridge_hexagon_center_angle_001.explanation: Central angle of hexagon = 360° ÷ 6 = 60°. Area = (1/2) × 6² × sin(60°) = 9√3 cm².

solution_steps.description: Each central triangle is isosceles with vertex angle 60°
solution_steps.calculation: Triangle type: isosceles with vertex angle 60°

solution_steps.description: Area of triangle = (1/2) × r² × sin(vertex angle)

why_wrong_explanations.0: You may have used the wrong formula or incorrect trigonometric value.
why_wrong_explanations.2: Check your calculation of the area formula for the triangle.
why_wrong_explanations.3: This would be the area if the triangle was equilateral with side 6, but we need the central triangle.

rescue_intermediate_octagon_center_angle.subject: Mathematics
rescue_intermediate_octagon_center_angle.question_text: A regular octagon is inscribed in a circle of radius 10 cm. What is the measure of each central angle, and what is the side length of the octagon? (Use cos 45° = √2/2)
rescue_intermediate_octagon_center_angle.explanation: Central angle = 360° ÷ 8 = 45°. Using cosine law: side = r√(2 − 2cos45°) = 10√(2 − √2) cm.

solution_steps.description: Use cosine law: side² = r² + r² - 2r²cos(central angle)

why_wrong_explanations.1: 10√2 is not correct — you need to apply the cosine law with the central angle of 45°.
why_wrong_explanations.2: 60° is the central angle for a hexagon, not an octagon. An octagon has 8 sides.
why_wrong_explanations.3: 30° would be for a 12-sided polygon. And the side length calculation is different.

pre_jee_octagon_area_001.subject: Mathematics
pre_jee_octagon_area_001.question_text: A regular octagon ABCDEFGH is inscribed in a circle of radius r. Express the area of the octagon in terms of r.
pre_jee_octagon_area_001.explanation: Area of regular octagon = 8 × (1/2) × r² × sin(45°) = 4r²sin(45°) = 4r²(√2/2) = 2√2 r².

solution_steps.description: A regular octagon inscribed in a circle can be divided into 8 identical triangles from the center
Each triangle has two sides equal to radius r and vertex angle = 360°/8 = 45°
Area of each triangle = (1/2) × r × r × sin(45°) = (r²/2)(√2/2) = r²√2/4

why_wrong_explanations.0: 2r²√2 and 2√2 r² are the same expression. Check the option carefully.
why_wrong_explanations.2: r²(1 + √2) is a different formula. The octagon area uses sin(45°), not (1 + √2).
why_wrong_explanations.3: 4r²sin(45°) = 4r²(√2/2) = 2√2 r², which is the same as option B.

jee_math_regular_polygon_001.subject: Mathematics
jee_math_regular_polygon_001.question_text: The interior angle of a regular polygon is 150°. How many diagonals does the polygon have?

solution_steps.description: Use the interior angle formula: (n-2) × 180° / n = 150°

why_wrong_explanations.0: 27 = 54/2. You may have divided by 2 an extra time.
why_wrong_explanations.1: 35 would be for a 10-sided polygon. Check your calculation of n from the interior angle.
why_wrong_explanations.3: 90 would be the number of diagonals for a 15-sided polygon.
